guide.std_overview_desc: At WIM UI, we maintain strict standards for both design and implementation to provide a consistent user experience. This page serves as an index to oversee these standardization documents and understand our design philosophy.
guide.std_pillar_1_desc: Provides a unified look and feel across all components, minimizing user learning costs.
guide.std_pillar_2_desc: Ensures equal access for all users through compliance with WAI-ARIA and full keyboard support.
guide.std_pillar_3_desc: Makes responses to operations clear, correctly conveying system and user states.
guide.std_cat_input_desc: Standards to enable users to operate intuitively without confusion.
guide.std_cat_display_desc: Standards for organizing information and conveying meaning accurately.
guide.std_cat_feedback_desc: Standards for providing appropriate feedback on system status to users.
guide.std_cat_layout_desc: Standards that define the overall structure and consistent placement of applications.
guide.std_next_steps_desc: Individual standardization documents summarize component-by-component comparisons, accessibility status, and specific Do/Don'ts. Always refer to the corresponding standard when adding new components or modifying existing ones.
guide.label_std_desc: Defines common rules for components that supplement information attributes or states, such as badges, tags, and chips.
guide.label_std_concept_desc: Ensures users can visually organize information by choosing the appropriate component based on the type of information and the presence of interaction.
guide.label_std_rule_2_desc: Unifies semantic colors (Success, Warning, Error, etc.) to ensure consistent meaning of information.
guide.label_std_rule_3_desc: Differentiates usage: Badges/Tags are non-interactive, while Chips are interactive (removable/selectable).
guide.label_std_rule_4_desc: When using icons, combine them with label text or provide appropriate Aria attributes.
guide.label_std_note_desc: Use 'Badge' for numerical counts, 'Tag' for information classification, and 'Chip' for manipulating choices.
guide.list_item_std_desc: When displaying user-defined options (dropdown items, menus, comboboxes, etc.), use the internal component BaseListItem to maintain a consistent look & feel (padding, hover color, click hit area) across the entire product.
guide.list_item_std_reason_desc: Previously, each component manually implemented items via div or li, leading to inconsistent hit areas and spacing between icons and text. Unifying these improves the 'tangibility' of the UI and the overall product quality.
guide.list_item_std_rule_1_desc: Do not specify padding or background-color (hover) in custom SCSS.
guide.list_item_std_rule_2_desc: Use src/components/_internal/BaseListItem when rendering selectable items.
guide.list_item_std_rule_3_desc: Ensure --wim-color-bg-hover is applied for hover states (automatically handled by BaseListItem).
guide.list_item_std_rules_list: The following rules apply when rendering selectable list items. Do not implement custom padding, hover styles, or click areas — use BaseListItem to ensure consistency.
guide.list_item_std_note_desc: If BaseListItem does not meet your requirements, consult the architecture team before implementing a custom solution.
guide.range_std_desc: Defines common rules for components where a user selects values from within a specific range, such as sliders and calendars.
guide.range_std_concept_desc: Provides a UI that allows intuitive manipulation of continuous value sets and enables accurate value entry and verification.
guide.range_std_rule_1_desc: Manage `value` (single value) or `range` (start/end value pair) in a consistent prop format.
guide.range_std_rule_2_desc: Set `min` (minimum) and `max` (maximum) to restrict selections within the valid range.
guide.range_std_rule_3_desc: Present current selection values in real-time through tooltips or numeric displays during dragging or selection.
guide.range_std_rule_4_desc: Consider fine-tuning with keyboard arrow keys and combining with direct input (where applicable).
guide.selection_std_desc: Defines common rules for components where a user selects one (or more) from several options, such as tabs and segmented controls.
guide.selection_std_concept_desc: Achieves smooth view switching and filtering by enhancing the visibility of the selected state and ensuring accessibility, including keyboard operations.
guide.selection_std_rule_1_desc: Use `value` and `onChange` (or `onValueChange`) to manage the current selection state.
guide.selection_std_rule_2_desc: Apply sliders or background color changes to the selected item to clearly indicate the active one.
guide.selection_std_rule_3_desc: Support moving between items with arrow keys and making selections with the Enter/Space keys.
guide.selection_std_rule_4_desc: Set `tablist` (for switching tabs) or `radiogroup` (for selecting settings) appropriately based on context.
guide.sequence_std_desc: Defines common rules for components that communicate ordered information or progress, such as Stepper and Timeline.
guide.sequence_std_concept_desc: Visualizes the flow of information in chronological or process order, allowing users to intuitively grasp 'where they are now' and 'what lies in the past or future.'
guide.sequence_std_rule_1_desc: Link items with lines (Connectors) to explicitly show information continuity.
guide.sequence_std_rule_2_desc: Express the status of each step (Wait, Process, Finish, Error) using colors and icons for easy identification of progress.
guide.sequence_std_rule_3_desc: Support both horizontal and vertical directions, allowing selection based on screen space and information density.
guide.sequence_std_rule_4_desc: Each item basic configuration consists of three elements: 'Icon/Point', 'Title', and 'Description'.
guide.state_std_desc: Defines common rules for components that communicate specific screen-wide states, such as EmptyState and Result.
guide.state_std_concept_desc: Presents information so that users can correctly perceive the current system status and decide what to do next without hesitation.
guide.state_std_rule_1_desc: Organize information in the order of Title, Description, and Action Button to optimize eye-tracking.
guide.state_std_rule_2_desc: Use icons and colors (Success, Error, Info, etc.) appropriate for the situation, ensuring the meaning is clear without relying on language alone.
guide.state_std_rule_3_desc: Offer means for users to solve problems or continue working through the `action` or `extra` properties.
guide.state_std_rule_4_desc: Allow translation keys for titles and descriptions to facilitate multi-language support.
guide.styling_std_desc: Guidelines on distinguishing between SCSS variables and CSS custom properties, and rules for token management in WIM UI.
guide.styling_std_concept_desc: Static logic is managed via SCSS, while themes and runtime extensibility are managed via CSS custom properties (Design Tokens).
guide.styling_std_rule_scss_desc: Used for defining primitive values, mathematical calculations (Type Scale, spacing multipliers), and static constants used only within the library.
guide.styling_std_rule_css_desc: All design elements (colors, spacing, fonts, border widths, etc.) must be exported as CSS variables. This enables dark mode support and runtime theme overrides.
guide.styling_std_rule_component_desc: Within individual component stylesheets (.scss), use var() whenever possible. Avoid hardcoded values (1px, 2rem, etc.) and reference standardized tokens instead.
guide.range_std_note_desc: Components with Range in their name (RangeSlider, RangeCalendar) accept an array of two values. Single-value variants accept a scalar. Do not mix these types.
guide.selection_std_note_desc: Choose the component whose ARIA role matches the semantic intent. Use Tabs for content switching, SegmentedControl for mutually exclusive settings, and ToggleGroup for multi-select filtering.
guide.sequence_std_note_desc: Stepper represents active user progress (steps to complete), while Timeline displays past events in chronological order. Do not swap these roles.
guide.state_std_note_desc: EmptyState and Result share the same content hierarchy via StatusContent internally. Dropzone does not support an action prop — use surrounding layout for call-to-action.
